tenders.title: Appointment of Project Management Consultant (PMC) for the Smart City Proposal (Chandigarh Smart City Limited)
tenders.scopeSummary: PMC to assist the Smart City SPV in developing, planning, designing, managing, implementing and monitoring the Smart City Proposal (area-based development plus pan-city projects). Time-based, performance-linked engagement covering project structuring, procurement support, supervision and quality assurance.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience in urban infrastructure / smart-city advisory or PMC assignments; office in India; not blacklisted
other: Company incorporated in India under Companies Act
For JV/consortium each member min avg turnover INR 250 Cr

technicalCriteria.criterion: Specific experience of the consultant relevant to the assignment
Adequacy and quality of proposed methodology and work plan (incl. presentation)
Qualifications and competence of key personnel

tenders.sourceDocument: RFP No. 1/2017, Chandigarh Smart City Limited (hosted on SmartNet NIUA)

tenders.title: Selection of Project Management Consultant (PMC) for Puducherry Smart City
tenders.scopeSummary: PMC for design, DPR preparation, supervision and monitoring of Smart City area-based and pan-city projects covering urban infrastructure (roads/mobility, ICT, e-governance, affordable housing/slum improvement, energy). Firm supports PSCDL across the project lifecycle.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience as PMC / design-DPR / supervision consultant across urban infra, mobility, ICT, housing (assignments of at least ₹100 lakh each scored per project)
other: Min avg annual turnover ₹30 Cr (single) / ₹10 Cr per consortium member

technicalCriteria.criterion: Specific experience of the consultant firm relevant to the assignment
Proposed methodology and work plan (with Team Leader presentation)
Qualifications and competence of key professional staff
technicalCriteria.notes: Balance of the 100 marks allocated to key personnel

tenders.title: Appointment of Smart City Infrastructure Project Management Consultants (Strategic Advisory & Smart Infrastructure PM Support)
tenders.issuingAuthority: New Delhi Municipal Council Smart City Limited (NDMCSCL)
tenders.scopeSummary: Strategic advisory and smart infrastructure project management support to NDMCSCL for ~24 months, with a 19-member core+non-core team (Project Director, Procurement, Financial, Spatial Urban Planner, Urban Infrastructure, etc.). Includes bid advisory, financial modelling and feasibility for water/transport/affordable-housing/parking sub-projects.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least one project (>₹10 Cr contract value) of project management/monitoring for an ULB in India; bid advisory experience on SCP-listed cities
other: Avg annual consulting revenue of Lead Member ≥ ₹250 Cr (last 3 FY)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Financial strength (avg annual consulting revenue of Lead Member)
Technical capability of firm/consortium (bid advisory on SCP projects)
subCriteria: 5 marks per bid-advisory experience related to SCP-listed cities

technicalCriteria.criterion: Key personnel (Project Director 8, Procurement 5, Sustainability 5, Financial 5, Spatial Urban Planner 5, Urban Infra 5, plus non-core)
technicalCriteria.notes: CVs not meeting desired qualification are not marked

tenders.title: Appointment of Project Management Consultant (PMC) for the Smart City Proposal, Nashik
tenders.issuingAuthority: Nashik Municipal Smart City Development Corporation Limited (NMSCDCL)
tenders.scopeSummary: PMC to assist NMSCDCL in developing, planning, designing, managing, implementing and monitoring the Smart City Proposal of Nashik. Time-based engagement linked with performance, covering area-based development and pan-city smart solutions.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Relevant experience in similar smart-city / urban infrastructure assignments (per Data Sheet)
other: Qualification documents pass/fail before technical scoring

technicalCriteria.criterion: Specific experience of the consultant firm relevant to the assignment
Adequacy of methodology, work plan and presentation
Qualifications and competence of key personnel
subCriteria: Adequacy / relevant sector experience (80% of position marks)

tenders.title: Selection of PMC to Design, Develop, Manage & Implement the Integrated Command & Control Centre (ICCC), CCTV, OFC & IT-enabled Infrastructure
tenders.scopeSummary: PMC to provide end-to-end planning, design, vendor coordination, implementation monitoring, quality assurance and capacity building for an Integrated Command & Control Centre, city-wide CCTV surveillance, an OFC network and related IT-enabled smart infrastructure under the Smart City initiative.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Demonstrable experience managing similar large-scale urban infrastructure and ICT/ICCC projects; pass pre-qualification stage

technicalCriteria.criterion: Firm experience / technical capability (post pre-qualification)
technicalCriteria.notes: Total technical = 100; min 70 to qualify. Municipal Commissioner may lower threshold if no bidder qualifies.

technicalCriteria.criterion: Key personnel qualifications
Approach, methodology & presentation-cum-demonstration

tenders.sourceDocument: RFP for Selection of PMC for ICCC, Bhavnagar Municipal Corporation (BMC/COMPUTER/01/2025-26)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Preparation of Area Development Plan & Catalytic Project Strategy for the Vizag Bay City
tenders.issuingAuthority: Visakhapatnam Metropolitan Region Development Authority (VMRDA), Dept. of Municipal Administration & Urban Development, Govt. of Andhra Pradesh
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultant to prepare an Area Development Plan and Catalytic Project Strategy for the Vizag Bay City, including GIS base-map preparation, stakeholder consultation, SWOC analysis, infrastructure cost estimation, disaster-resilience/climate planning, and smart/digital infrastructure planning under a land-pooling/PPP framework.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience preparing area development / master plans and integrated infrastructure plans for large urban areas

technicalCriteria.criterion: Consultant's relevant experience (area development / master planning)
Proposed key experts
Methodology & approach for the Area Development Plan

tenders.sourceDocument: RFP for Selection of Consultant for Area Development Plan, Vizag Bay City (VMRDA)

tenders.title: Selection of Transaction Advisor & Project Management Consultant for Underground Commercial Complex with Theme Park at Nagababa Khatal (PPP)
tenders.scopeSummary: Transaction Advisory plus PMC to conceptualize the project, prepare a comprehensive DPR, structure and select a developer on PPP (finance-build-operate-maintain), provide financial/technical advisory, and supervise/monitor construction of an underground commercial complex with a theme park (~4 acre, ~₹100 Cr).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Transaction Advisory for 2+ PPP projects (>₹20 Cr each) AND PMC for 2+ civil infrastructure projects (>₹20 Cr each) in India
other: Min avg annual consultancy turnover ₹10 Cr (last 3 FY)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Project Experience
subCriteria: 1A: Transaction Advisory for PPP urban infra projects (5 marks/assignment >₹20 Cr)
1B: PMC for civil infra projects (5 marks/assignment >₹20 Cr)
1C: TA+PMC of urban infra in Jharkhand (5 marks/assignment >₹10 Cr)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Key personnel and methodology

tenders.sourceDocument: RFP for Transaction Advisor & PMC, Nagababa Khatal, Ranchi Municipal Corporation

tenders.title: Appointment of Independent Engineer / PMC for Municipal Solid Waste Management Project at Berhampur Municipal Corporation (DBOT)
tenders.issuingAuthority: Odisha Urban Infrastructure Development Fund (OUIDF) for Berhampur Municipal Corporation
tenders.scopeSummary: Independent Engineer / PMC / construction supervision consultant for an integrated Municipal Solid Waste Management project implemented on Design-Build-Operate-Transfer (DBOT) mode. KfW-assisted; consultant supervises the operator and certifies performance across the project period.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: One similar project with fee ≥ ₹25 lakh (or ongoing 1-year engagement) as IE / PMC / Authority's Engineer / Construction Supervision in last 10 years
other: Joint avg annual turnover ≥ ₹3 Cr from consultancy (last 3 FY)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Bidder's experience in similar assignments
Qualification & experience of key personnel
Approach & methodology / work plan (with presentation)

tenders.sourceDocument: RFP Notice No. 05/OUIDF-178/2017 (Berhampur SWM Independent Engineer)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for State-Level Programme Management Unit (PMU) for Solid & Liquid Waste Management under SBM 2.0 (Urban)
tenders.issuingAuthority: Department of Urban Development & Housing, Government of Arunachal Pradesh
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultant to run a State-Level Programme Management Unit supporting implementation of Solid & Liquid Waste Management under Swachh Bharat Mission 2.0 (Urban) across 47 ULBs — preparing plans, validating DPRs, procurement assistance, project development and implementation support, ODF/GFC star-rating hand-holding.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: SWM / sanitation PMU or advisory experience; key personnel with SWM domain experience
keyPersonnel: Team Leader cum Programme Management Expert
other: Min avg annual revenue ≥ ₹50 Cr from consultancy (last 3 FY)
Positive net worth & profit last 3 FY

technicalCriteria.criterion: Relevant experience of the firm (SWM)
Relevant experience of the team (key experts by years of SWM experience)
Proposed approach & methodology / presentation

tenders.title: GIS-Based Master Plan Formulation for AMRUT City - Roorkee
tenders.issuingAuthority: State Mission Directorate, Urban Development, Government of Uttarakhand
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultancy firm to prepare a GIS-based Draft Master Plan for Roorkee under AMRUT (sub-scheme: GIS-based Master Plan Formulation), per the Uttarakhand Urban & Country Planning & Development Act 1973 — demand assessment, issue identification, projected requirements, development strategy and draft proposals on GIS base maps with sector-wise data analysis.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience preparing GIS-based master/development plans; plan formulation using GIS database

technicalCriteria.criterion: Specific experience of the consulting firm related to the assignment
Adequacy of proposed work plan & methodology in response to TOR
Qualifications & competence of key professional staff

tenders.sourceDocument: Uttarakhand GIS-based Master Plan RFP (AMRUT), Roorkee - Least Cost Selection method

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Vision & Master Plan, Detailed Layout Plan, Urban Design Guidelines, Integrated Infrastructure Plan & Preliminary Engineering Design for Sector 29, Gurugram
tenders.scopeSummary: Master-planner-cum-engineering firm to prepare a Vision & Master Plan, detailed layout plan, urban design guidelines, integrated infrastructure plan (roads, water, power, ICT, SWM) and preliminary engineering design for Sector 29, Gurugram — including drone topographic survey, GIS base map, stakeholder consultation and SWOC analysis.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Master planning / urban design assignments; Team Leader with relevant completed projects
other: Avg annual turnover ≥ ₹50 Cr per annum (each of last 3 FY)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Average turnover of applicant
Key personnel (Team Leader & experts)
Approach & methodology (with presentation)

tenders.sourceDocument: HSVP Sector 29 Gurugram Master Plan RFP (Revised 01.07.2024)

tenders.title: Empanelment of Consultants / Architects for Technical Consultancy, Architectural Planning, DPR Preparation & Bid Process Management for Building & Infrastructure Works
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Empanelment (Group A ≤₹10 Cr, Group B >₹10 Cr)
tenders.scopeSummary: Empanelment of consultants/architects to provide technical consultancy, architectural planning, design & estimation, DPR preparation and bid process management for building and civic infrastructure works, ensuring designs meet national standards/codes. Empanelment split into Group A (projects ≤ ₹10 Cr) and Group B (> ₹10 Cr).

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: In last 5 years, similar consultancy for Govt/Semi-Govt: 3 projects >₹2 Cr, or 2 projects >₹2.5 Cr, or 1 project >₹4 Cr
other: Min avg annual consultancy turnover ₹0.25 Cr (any 3 of last 5 FY)
Existing empaneled consultants under NIT USCL/342 need not re-apply

technicalCriteria.criterion: Technical capacity (similar completed building/infra consultancy projects, last 5 years)
subCriteria: Number and value of completed projects for Govt/Semi-Govt bodies

technicalCriteria.criterion: Financial capacity (avg annual consultancy turnover)

tenders.sourceDocument: NIT No. USCL/427, Ujjain Smart City Limited (Empanelment RFP 2026)

tenders.title: Selection of Agency for Establishment & Operation of State-Level & City-Level Technical Cells (SLTC/CLTC) under PMAY-U 2.0 (Housing for All)
tenders.issuingAuthority: Rajasthan Urban Drinking Water, Sewerage & Infrastructure Corporation Ltd (RUDSICO), Govt. of Rajasthan
tenders.scopeSummary: Agency to provide and manage manpower for a State-Level Technical Cell and City-Level Technical Cells supporting implementation of Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Urban 2.0 in Rajasthan — programme management, coordination, MIS, capacity building and technical support to the state mission across ULBs.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: ≥5 assignments providing technical manpower of similar nature to Govt/PSU (15 marks); years in business ≥10 (15 marks)
other: Avg turnover last 3 FY ≥ 30% of estimated value (~₹8.69 Cr)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Overall financial strength (avg turnover last 3 FY)
Previous work experience (years in business + similar manpower assignments)
Proposed methodology, work plan, risk assessment
Key professional staff qualification & competency (7 SLTC + 5 CLTC positions)

tenders.sourceDocument: RUDSICO RFP for SLTC/CLTC under PMAY-U 2.0, Rajasthan (Least Cost Selection method)

evaluationFramework.summary: Across urban and smart-city consultancy procurements, Indian authorities almost universally use Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS), with technical:financial weighting of 80:20 the clear default for planning, PMC and design assignments and 70:30 appearing on more IT/system-integration-flavoured smart-city work. A minority of assignments — typically standardized master-plan formulation (e.g. AMRUT GIS master plans) or manpower/technical-cell contracts (e.g. PMAY-U SLTC/CLTC) — use Least Cost Selection (LCS/L1) after a technical qualification gate. Every process is two-envelope: a pass/fail pre-qualification / eligibility screen (legal status, turnover, similar experience, non-blacklisting), then a scored technical evaluation out of 100. Financial envelopes are opened only for firms clearing the minimum technical score, which sits at 70 in most PMC/ICCC tenders, 75 in more competitive smart-city and DPR/independent-engineer selections, up to 80 for GIS master plans, and as low as 50-60 for solid-waste / IEC / manpower assignments.

criteria.label: Firm's Relevant Urban/Smart-City Experience
criteria.description: Number, value and relevance of similar completed/ongoing assignments (PMC, DPR, master planning, transaction advisory, supervision) for government/ULB clients, usually within the last 5-10 years. Most tenders award fixed marks per qualifying assignment above a value threshold (commonly ₹20 Cr project cost or ₹100 lakh consultancy fee), with bonus weight for same-sector or same-state experience.

scoringGuidance.meaning: 5+ similar urban assignments of comparable/greater value, including same sub-sector and government clients
3-4 similar assignments meeting the value threshold
No directly comparable assignment above the value threshold

inputFields.label: Number of similar urban/smart-city assignments completed (last 7 years)
inputFields.scoring: Most RFPs give ~5 marks per qualifying assignment; 3+ typically needed for a strong score, 5+ for full marks.

inputFields.label: Value of largest comparable assignment (project cost or consultancy fee, INR)
inputFields.scoring: Assignment must exceed the RFP threshold (often ₹20 Cr project cost or ₹1 Cr consultancy fee) to be counted.

inputFields.label: Do you have experience in the exact sub-sector (e.g. ICCC, MSW, PMAY, master planning)?
inputFields.scoring: Same sub-sector / same-state experience attracts bonus marks in most tenders.

criteria.label: Financial Strength / Average Annual Turnover
criteria.description: Average annual turnover (usually from consultancy/professional fees) over the last 3 financial years. Used both as a pass/fail eligibility gate (thresholds range from ₹0.25 Cr for small design empanelments to ₹250-500 Cr for large smart-city PMCs, or a % of estimated value such as 30-50%) and, in many tenders, as scored marks on a turnover slab.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Turnover well above the top slab defined in the RFP
Turnover comfortably above the minimum, in a mid slab
Turnover just meets the minimum eligibility threshold
Below minimum turnover - proposal disqualified at eligibility

inputFields.label: Average annual turnover, last 3 financial years (INR)
inputFields.scoring: Compared to the RFP minimum (fixed amount or % of estimated value). Higher slabs earn 5-30 marks where turnover is scored.

inputFields.label: Is the turnover from consultancy / professional fees (not total revenue)?
inputFields.scoring: Many urban RFPs require consultancy-specific turnover, CA/UDIN-certified.

criteria.label: Approach, Methodology, Work Plan & Presentation
criteria.description: Quality of the technical approach, understanding of the TOR, work plan, staffing/manning schedule, comments on TOR, and (very commonly) a live presentation by the proposed Team Leader. Weight ranges from 10 marks (classic MoHUA PMC model) to 30 marks (SWM/IEC and master-plan tenders, often including a scored presentation).

scoringGuidance.meaning: Clear, TOR-responsive methodology; realistic implementable work plan; balanced staffing; strong presentation
Sound methodology with minor gaps; workable plan
Generic methodology partially responsive to TOR
Vague or non-responsive approach; weak work plan

inputFields.label: Self-assessed strength of your methodology & work plan vs the TOR
inputFields.scoring: Maps to the excellent/good/fair/poor bands; 'fully tailored' targets full methodology marks.

inputFields.label: Will your proposed Team Leader personally deliver the technical presentation?
inputFields.scoring: Team-Leader-led presentations are expected/scored in most urban PMC and planning tenders.

criteria.label: Key Personnel (Team Leader & Experts)
criteria.description: Qualifications and competence of proposed key experts, scored per position on education, relevant experience (years) and adequacy for the role. This is usually the single largest block (35-55 marks) in urban PMC/planning tenders. Team Leader CV commonly must independently score a minimum (e.g. 75%); CVs failing minimum qualification may score zero.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All required experts proposed, each exceeding minimum experience; Team Leader well above threshold
Full team; most experts exceed minimum experience
Team present but several experts only just meet minimums
Missing key positions or CVs below minimum qualification (risk of proposal failure)

inputFields.label: Team Leader's total relevant experience (years)
inputFields.scoring: Experience bands drive marks (e.g. >12 yrs = top band). Team Leader CV often must score ≥75% or the whole proposal fails.

inputFields.label: Number of required key experts you can propose meeting the TOR minimums
inputFields.scoring: Each named position (e.g. Urban Planner, GIS Expert, PPP/Financial Expert, SWM Expert) carries fixed marks; missing positions lose marks.

inputFields.label: Share of key experts with a postgraduate qualification
inputFields.scoring: Per-expert scoring commonly splits ~40% qualification / 60% experience; postgraduate degrees earn the qualification marks.

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (Updated June 2022), MoF, Dept. of Expenditure
keyPolicies.relevance: Governs consultancy procurement under GFR 2017. Defines selection methods - QCBS (Sec 3.9), Least Cost Selection (Sec 3.8), Single Source (3.10) and Fixed Budget - and the two-envelope, minimum-technical-score process that every urban RFP follows.

keyPolicies.name: Model Tender Document for Procurement of Consultancy Services, MoF (CPPP)
keyPolicies.relevance: Standard REoI/RFP template (EOI shortlisting then RFP) with QCBS/LCS/FBS/SSS selection options and standard qualification and evaluation forms mirrored by most ULB and SPV tenders.

keyPolicies.name: Smart City Mission Guidelines, MoUD/MoHUA
keyPolicies.relevance: Establishes the SPV-led delivery model, empanelment of consulting firms by MoUD, template PMC RFPs in the Mission Toolkit, and the SCP evaluation criteria that shape smart-city PMC scope and consultant selection.
